Author: mole99
Content:
Everyone who was offered a free slot will receive CoB bonded chips for free (as discussed with Tim). However, it is unclear how many bonded chips you will receive, and you may be able to get more if you pay a little extra.

A pack of open source chips would be interesting :)
However, it would probably be without the host-PCBs, since wafer.space only bonds the chips to the CoB PCB.

Author: rebelmike
Content:
Awesome!

And yes it would need to be a community effort to circulate host PCBs (either designs or swapping fabbed boards with each other). Though maybe you could get quite far with a breakout and some breadboarding?

Author: chvsnaveen12
Content: @Leo Moser (mole99) are there still some free slots left? Would love to submit a design before purchasing a slot on the next shuttle.

Author: mole99
Content: You would need to ask @Tim 'mithro' Ansell about that. However, please keep in mind that the deadline is already this Wednesday.

Author: chvsnaveen12
Content: I am a bit new to this, but I suppose if I have a well verified RTL the tools will do most of the heavy lifting?

Author: mole99
Content: Yes. You can simply replace the placeholder RTL in: https://github.com/wafer-space/gf180mcu-project-template

Author: rebelmike
Content: I would advise though that even starting with good RTL the deadline is extremely close.  Remember synthesis and prechecks will take hours so you don't have many iterations to get it right.  Start right away if you're thinking of trying to get something in!
